title: Nailstone 43-37776 Memorial
crew_summary: 13 killed including pilot 1Lt. Michael H. Primus
narrative: The B-17 "Heavenly Body" was on a night navigation training flight from its home base at Eye in Suffolk — a long way from the operational missions over Germany that the 490th Bomb Group normally flew. Twelve men were aboard, including three passengers along for the ride. Over NW Leicestershire, while executing a turn near Nailstone, the nose dropped and the port wing struck the ground. The aircraft was completely destroyed by fire and explosion. All twelve men were killed. Five were subsequently buried at Cambridge American Cemetery at Madingley, near Cambridge; the other seven were repatriated to the United States after the war. This was the single deadliest crash in Leicestershire during the war. The "Heavenly Body" name and the fact that these men died on a training flight far from their base — not in combat — adds to the tragedy.

crew.name: Michael H. Primus
crew.burial_location: 5 buried at Cambridge American Cemetery; 7 repatriated to USA
crew.fate_detail: Killed when the B-17 struck a tree during a low-altitude turn near Nailstone and exploded. All twelve aboard died.
crew.bio: First Lieutenant and pilot of the B-17 'Heavenly Body', 490th Bomb Group.

cause_of_loss: Nose dropped during turn; port wing struck ground; destroyed by fire and explosion
memorial_details: A memorial stands in the Memorial Garden in the Church Gardens at Nailstone, on the A447 between Nailstone and Ibstock. The memorial commemorates all twelve men and is maintained by the local community. It is the only memorial to American aircrew in the East Midlands region covered by this report.

external_narratives.summary: US War Memorials database entry for the B-17 'Heavenly Body' (43-37776) memorial at Nailstone. Documents the stone of remembrance with attached plaque in the Memorial Garden commemorating all 12 crew members of the 490th Bomb Group.

Detailed technical and historical account of the B-17G 43-37776 crash at Nailstone on 27 September 1944. The bomber was on a training flight from Eye to Liverpool when it flew low over Barlestone and Nailstone, hit a tree and exploded. All twelve aboard were killed.
key_details: Training flight route was Eye to Liverpool
Hit a tree on farmland near Nailstone
Fully loaded with bombs and ammunition

external_narratives.summary: International B-17 database entry for 43-37776 'Heavenly Body', confirming the aircraft details, unit assignment to the 490th Bomb Group, and loss circumstances at Nailstone.
